items.title: Eight in 10 Malaysians Unaware Of Sight‑Threatening Retinal Diseases, Survey Finds
items.summary: As Malaysia moves towards becoming an aged nation, with NCDs such as diabetes continuing to affect many Malaysians, preserving functional independence must be an important public health priority. items.summary: Malaysia’s two-tiered health care system has deep roots in more than three decades of service, but it must now prove its flexibility. The post Three-Phase Rescue: Saving Malaysia’s Health Care System Before The Flatline — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Phase Out Parallel Pathway Programmes After UK’s Pivot
items.summary: With RCSEd now calling the cardiothoracic surgery parallel pathway a "Malaysian qualification", after the UK's new law, Malaysia should start phasing out the parallel pathway. Expand local Master's clinical programmes to private universities and hospitals. The post Phase Out Parallel Pathway Programmes After UK’s Pivot appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Private Dental Practitioners Facing Growing Financial, Professional Pressure — MPDPA
items.summary: Young dentists today are facing mounting challenges including income instability, rising rental and staffing costs, increasing prices of dental materials and equipment, and competitive fees undercutting within dense markets. items.title: What Comes After The Crash: Be Aware Of The Head Injury You Cannot See
items.summary: Road accident-related head injuries may unfold beyond the crash itself, with delayed symptoms and internal complications that are not always visible at the scene. The post What Comes After The Crash: Be Aware Of The Head Injury You Cannot See appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Vital Signs Of Malaysian Health Care Are Failing — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: The confluence of an unprecedented manpower exodus, a proposed RM4.65 bil funding cut, and the highest medical inflation in a decade has pushed MOH to its breaking point. Without radical, immediate intervention, Malaysia risks becoming a cautionary tale. items.title: Target 45-Hour Work Week For Health Workers Beyond Housemen: MMA
items.summary: MMA wants Malaysia to eventually move to a 45-hour work week for all health workers beyond housemen. Ex-senator Dr Lingeshwaran says the rigid 3-shift system for HOs will cause medical officers and specialists to be overworked due to a housemen shortage. The post Target 45-Hour Work Week For Health Workers Beyond Housemen: MMA appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cardiothoracic Surgery Parallel Pathway Produces ‘Malaysian Qualification’: RCSEd
items.summary: After the UK's new Medical Training (Prioritisation) Act became law, the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh now says the cardiothoracic surgery parallel pathway is a "Malaysian qualification". But Malaysia's Medical Act lists it as a UK qualification. The post Cardiothoracic Surgery Parallel Pathway Produces ‘Malaysian Qualification’: RCSEd appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: UK Education ‘Important’, Says British High Comm Amid NUMed Limbo
items.summary: Despite 103 NUMed graduates being stuck in a limbo due to the UK's new medical training law, the British High Commission says the UK will continue to promote UK education in Malaysia because education is an important pillar of the UK-Malaysia relationship. The post UK Education ‘Important’, Says British High Comm Amid NUMed Limbo appeared first on CodeBlue .
